mapping.a_element: the pendulum as an oscillatory element
mapping.b_element: the NF-kB core reaction network as an oscillatory element

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is coupling-induced synchronization of two oscillators, where an instantaneous bidirectional coupling and small length detuning drive a transition to phase-locking; Paper B's machinery is a single biochemical limit-cycle that generates periodic spikes plus downstream ultrasensitive gene regulation (Hill coefficients >20). The only overlap is the generic word 'oscillations' — there is no coupling/synchronization in B and no threshold-based ultrasensitive readout in A, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving process holds.
